# Debugging

## Atom index (8 atoms, auto-generated from atoms.jsonl)

_Every atom under this topic with its source proof link. Regenerate with `python -m engram topics`._

### claim (2)
- **Debugging is mostly a process of elimination, not a mystery.** — [ada-debugging](https://example.com/ada/debugging) `[CORE]`
- **The fastest debuggers read the entire error message before touching anything.** — [ada-debugging](https://example.com/ada/debugging) `[CORE]`

### concept (1)
- **The bug lives between the input you trust and the output you don't; the job is to keep cutting that space in half.** — [ada-debugging](https://example.com/ada/debugging) `[CORE]`

### method (2)
- **Form one hypothesis you can actually test rather than spraying five changes and hoping one sticks.** — [ada-debugging](https://example.com/ada/debugging) `[CORE]`
- **When you find the bug, write the test that would have caught it, so it can never quietly come back.** — [ada-debugging](https://example.com/ada/debugging) `[CORE]`

### analogy (1)
- **Debugging is like a binary search over the space between trusted input and wrong output — halve it each step.** — [ada-debugging](https://example.com/ada/debugging) `[STATED]`

### opinion (1)
- **Print statements aren't shameful: a print in the right place beats an hour in a fancy debugger in the wrong place.** — [ada-debugging](https://example.com/ada/debugging) `[STATED]`

### warning (1)
- **Guessing feels fast, but a wrong guess costs you a change, a rerun, and a little of your confidence.** — [ada-debugging](https://example.com/ada/debugging) `[STATED]`
